steps.title: Find the balance after the first 4 months
steps.content:
Original balance: 800.00 dollars. After 4 months it increased by $0.5\%$ of the original:
$$800.00 \times 1.005 = 804.00$$
So at month $4$, the balance is 804.00 dollars. This is the starting balance for the lower-rate period.

steps.title: Identify the periodic growth
steps.content:
From month $4$ onward, every $2$ months the balance grows by an additional $0.2\%$ of the balance $2$ months prior. That means each $2$-month period multiplies the balance by $1.002$:
  • Month $6$: $804.00 \times 1.002$
  • Month $8$: $804.00 \times (1.002)^2$
  • Month $10$: $804.00 \times (1.002)^3$
For $x$ months after opening (with $x \geq 4$), the number of $2$-month periods since month $4$ is $\frac{x-4}{2}$, giving:
$$B(x) = 804.00(1.002)^{\frac{x-4}{2}}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $B(x)=804.00(1.002)^{\frac{x-4}{2}}$ — correct starting balance, 804.00 dollars, and correct exponent (one period per $2$ months)
  • B: $B(x)=800.00(1.002)^{\frac{x-4}{2}}$ — wrong starting balance; should be 804.00 dollars, not 800.00 dollars, since the special-rate interest already accrued
  • C: $B(x)=804.00(1.002)^{2(x-4)}$ — exponent is wrong; $\left(2\right)(x-4)$ compounds twice per month instead of once per $2$ months
  • D: $B(x)=800.00(1.002)^{2(x-4)}$ — wrong on both counts: starting balance and exponent
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
